Computation Models of Cognition

Aug 10, 2017 

Note : This post contains my notes from the CMC course at IITM 
       and also some views I have on the topic.

The idea of this post is discuss cognitive science & challenges in creating a human-level intelligence, study the current day tools/techniques we have and also discuss possible non-traditional methods.

CONTENTS

  • Preliminary Thoughts
    • Is brain the only place where the cognition happens?

Preliminary Thoughts

Is brain the only place where the cognition happens?

It is very true that our brain plays a very important role in cognition. However, there is the theory of Embodied cognition which indicates that the entire body of an organism shapes the cognitive abilities.

Say we had a very powerful computing machine and could simulate the neuron connections in the human brain, Can we generate natural-intelligence ? There is a gap in our understanding of how the brain works, but if we truely understand all the underlying physiology of human brain and emulate it on a machine is that sufficient for intelligence is a hard question to answer. Philosophically speaking it is possible there some meta-physical element is attached to human intelligence which we haven’t discovered. However, it is important to see what we can engineer than debate about philosophical aspects.

Also, there is question of Build vs Grow in Aritifical Intelligence. Human intelligence is shaped by experiences and memory, this enhances over time this is the “Growth of Intelligence”. However, many present day models for the task of language translation is a “Built Intelligence” we show it a lot of adult conversations/language during training, So the model has not learnt say the translation ability like how a human does.

“If our brains were simple enough for us to understand them, we’d be so simple that we couldn’t.”
– Ian Stewart, The Collapse of Chaos: Discovering Simplicity in a Complex World
